title: Fallen Land - Expansion - Descendants
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=a2n31BGGWFI

Daniel (@dungeondive) cracks the **Descendants** expansion, backed in the Fallen Land second-edition campaign and arriving via Amazon retail rather than Kickstarter — a big point in the publisher's favour for him. Most of what you're paying for, frankly, is *storage and quality of life*, and that turns out to be exactly what the base game needed.

The long box brings card dividers, plastic chip trays, and a card-storage compartment that lets the entire base game live inside the expansion box — setup time goes through the floor. On the gameplay side, the headline addition is the **Town Events deck**: 66 written event cards that replace the old d10 town-event roll, drawn at the start of a turn and resolved at the end, giving each month a thematic spine and pushing the writing-on-cards quality bar even higher. Twelve new alternate factions slot in alongside the originals (each token now hosts two factions, mutually exclusive), with two — the Collective and Los Centinelas — locked behind the second-edition map's new Mexican and Canadian regions.

Smaller additions: a new tier-2 town technology (build a resource inside your town), new spoils including the Mystery Machine and a B-25 Mitchell bomber, and a stack of quality-of-life chits for tracking links, NPCs, and special abilities at the table.

----

Daniel calls Descendants "a quality-of-life expansion that also brings content" — storage is doing as much work as cards. Have you ever bought an expansion primarily for the trays, sleeves, or organisational scaffolding rather than the rules?
